as_user.desc: (Legacy) Pass true to post the message as the authed user instead of as a bot. Defaults to false. Can only be used by classic apps. See legacy `as_user` parameter below.

attachments.desc: A JSON-based array of structured attachments, presented as a URL-encoded string. The `text` field is not enforced as required when using `blocks` or `attachments`.

blocks.desc: A JSON-based array of structured blocks, presented as a URL-encoded string. The usage of the `text` field changes depending on whether you're using `blocks`. The `text` field is not enforced as required when using `blocks` or `attachments`. However, we highly recommended that you include `text` to provide a fallback when using `blocks`, as described above. It is expected behavior that screen readers will default to the top-level `text` field of your post, and will not read the content of any interior `blocks` in the underlying structure of the message. do not include a top-level `text` field if the message has `blocks`, and allow Slack attempt to build it for you by appending content from supported `blocks` to be read by the screen reader.

channel.desc: An encoded ID or channel name that represents a channel, private group, or IM channel to send the message to. See below for more details.

current_draft_last_updated_ts.desc: This field represents the timestamp of the draft's last update at the time this API is called. If the current message is a draft, this field can be provided to ensure synchronization with the server.

icon_emoji.desc: Emoji to use as the icon for this message. Overrides `icon_url`.

icon_url.desc: URL to an image to use as the icon for this message.

link_names.desc: Find and link user groups. No longer supports linking individual users; use syntax shown in Mentioning Users instead.

markdown_text.desc: Accepts message text formatted in markdown. This argument should not be used in conjunction with `blocks` or `text`. Limit this field to 12,000 characters.

metadata.desc: JSON object with event\_type and event\_payload fields, presented as a URL-encoded string. You can also provide Work Object entity metadata using this parameter. Metadata you post to Slack is accessible to any app or user who is a member of that workspace.

mrkdwn.desc: Disable Slack markup parsing by setting to `false`. Enabled by default. The behavior of `parse` is different for text formatted with `mrkdwn`. By default, or when `parse` is set to `none`, `mrkdwn` formatting is implemented. To ignore `mrkdwn` formatting, set `parse` to `full`.

parse.desc: Change how messages are treated. See below. The behavior of `parse` is different for text formatted with `mrkdwn`. By default, or when `parse` is set to `none`, `mrkdwn` formatting is implemented. To ignore `mrkdwn` formatting, set `parse` to `full`.

reply_broadcast.desc: Used in conjunction with `thread_ts` and indicates whether reply should be made visible to everyone in the channel or conversation. Defaults to `false`.

text.desc: How this field works and whether it is required depends on other fields you use in your API call. See below for more detail. The usage of the `text` field changes depending on whether you're using `blocks`. The `text` field is not enforced as required when using `blocks` or `attachments`. However, we highly recommended that you include `text` to provide a fallback when using `blocks`, as described above. It is expected behavior that screen readers will default to the top-level `text` field of your post, and will not read the content of any interior `blocks` in the underlying structure of the message. do not include a top-level `text` field if the message has `blocks`, and allow Slack attempt to build it for you by appending content from supported `blocks` to be read by the screen reader.

thread_ts.desc: Provide another message's `ts` value to make this message a reply. Avoid using a reply's `ts` value; use its parent instead.

unfurl_links.desc: Pass true to enable unfurling of primarily text-based content. If you want to suppress link unfurls in messages containing Block Kit blocks, set `unfurl_links` and `unfurl_media` to false.

unfurl_media.desc: Pass false to disable unfurling of media content. If you want to suppress link unfurls in messages containing Block Kit blocks, set `unfurl_links` and `unfurl_media` to false.

desc: Sends a message to a channel.

errors.access_denied: Access to a resource specified in the request is denied.
errors.accesslimited: Access to this method is limited on the current network
errors.account_inactive: Authentication token is for a deleted user or workspace when using a `bot` token.
errors.as_user_not_supported: The `as_user` parameter does not function with workspace apps.
errors.attachment_payload_limit_exceeded: Attachment payload size is too long.
errors.cannot_reply_to_message: This message type cannot have thread replies.
errors.draft_already_deleted: The draft has already been deleted.
errors.draft_already_sent: The draft has already been sent.
errors.draft_has_conflict: The client draft version is out of sync with the server draft version.
errors.duplicate_message_not_found: No duplicate message exists associated with `client_msg_id`.
errors.ekm_access_denied: Your message couldn’t be sent because your admins have disabled sending messages to this channel.
errors.enterprise_is_restricted: The method cannot be called from an Enterprise.
errors.fatal_error: The server could not complete your operation(s) without encountering a catastrophic error. It's possible some aspect of the operation succeeded before the error was raised.
errors.internal_error: The server could not complete your operation(s) without encountering an error, likely due to a transient issue on our end. It's possible some aspect of the operation succeeded before the error was raised.
errors.invalid_arg_name: The method was passed an argument whose name falls outside the bounds of accepted or expected values. This includes very long names and names with non-alphanumeric characters other than `_`. If you get this error, it is typically an indication that you have made a _very_ malformed API call.
errors.invalid_arguments: The method was called with invalid arguments.
errors.invalid_array_arg: The method was passed an array as an argument. Please only input valid strings.
errors.invalid_auth: Some aspect of authentication cannot be validated. Either the provided token is invalid or the request originates from an IP address disallowed from making the request.
errors.invalid_blocks: Blocks submitted with this message are not valid.
errors.invalid_blocks_format: The `blocks` is not a valid JSON object or doesn't match the Block Kit syntax.
errors.invalid_charset: The method was called via a `POST` request, but the `charset` specified in the `Content-Type` header was invalid. Valid charset names are: `utf-8` `iso-8859-1`.
errors.invalid_form_data: The method was called via a `POST` request with `Content-Type` `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` or `multipart/form-data`, but the form data was either missing or syntactically invalid.
errors.invalid_post_type: The method was called via a `POST` request, but the specified `Content-Type` was invalid. Valid types are: `application/json` `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` `multipart/form-data` `text/plain`.
errors.markdown_text_conflict: Markdown text cannot be used in conjunction with `blocks` or `text` argument.
errors.message_limit_exceeded: Members on this team are sending too many messages. For more details, see https://slack.com/help/articles/115002422943-Usage-limits-for-free-workspaces.
errors.messages_tab_disabled: Messages tab for the app is disabled.
errors.metadata_must_be_sent_from_app: Message metadata can only be posted or updated using an app-level token.
errors.missing_file_data: Attempted to share a file but some required data was missing.
errors.missing_post_type: The method was called via a `POST` request and included a data payload, but the request did not include a `Content-Type` header.
errors.missing_scope: The token used is not granted the specific scope permissions required to complete this request.
errors.msg_blocks_too_long: Blocks submitted with this message are too long.
errors.no_permission: The workspace token used in this request does not have the permissions necessary to complete the request. Make sure your app is a member of the conversation it's attempting to post a message to.
errors.not_allowed_token_type: The token type used in this request is not allowed.
errors.not_in_channel: Cannot post user messages to a channel they are not in.
errors.org_login_required: The workspace is undergoing an enterprise migration and will not be available until migration is complete.
errors.rate_limited: Application has posted too many messages, read the Rate Limit documentation for more information.
errors.ratelimited: The request has been ratelimited. Refer to the `Retry-After` header for when to retry the request.
errors.request_timeout: The method was called via a `POST` request, but the `POST` data was either missing or truncated.
errors.restricted_action: A workspace preference prevents the authenticated user from posting.
errors.restricted_action_non_threadable_channel: Cannot post thread replies into a non\_threadable channel.
errors.restricted_action_read_only_channel: Cannot post any message into a read-only channel.
errors.restricted_action_thread_locked: Cannot post replies to a thread that has been locked by admins.
errors.restricted_action_thread_only_channel: Cannot post top-level messages into a thread-only channel.
errors.slack_connect_canvas_sharing_blocked: Admin has disabled Canvas File sharing in all Slack Connect communications.
errors.slack_connect_file_link_sharing_blocked: Admin has disabled Slack File sharing in all Slack Connect communications.
errors.slack_connect_lists_sharing_blocked: Admin has disabled Lists sharing in all Slack Connect communications.
errors.team_access_not_granted: The token used is not granted the specific workspace access required to complete this request.
errors.team_added_to_org: The workspace associated with your request is currently undergoing migration to an Enterprise Organization. Web API and other platform operations will be intermittently unavailable until the transition is complete.
errors.team_not_found: This error occurs if, when using an org-wide token, the `channel_name` is passed instead of the `channel_id`.
errors.token_revoked: Authentication token is for a deleted user or workspace or the app has been removed when using a `user` token.
errors.too_many_attachments: Too many attachments were provided with this message. A maximum of 100 attachments are allowed on a message.
errors.too_many_contact_cards: Too many contact\_cards were provided with this message. A maximum of 10 contact cards are allowed on a message.
